dimensions.label: Clinical Practice & Patient Care
dimensions.description: Patient care, medical decisions, clinic operations, and clinical knowledge management.
dimensions.why_it_matters: As a practicing physician, cognitive exhaustion by end-of-day leaves little capacity for critical patient communications. Missing an urgent message can have serious consequences.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Evidence-based medicine requires continuous learning. The average physician spends 2 hours on EHR for every 1 hour of patient care (Sinsky et al., 2016). AI-assisted triage can recover 40% of this administrative time.
key_insights: Priority scoring reduces cognitive load by 60%
Literature alerts keep knowledge current without manual searching
Decision support augments (not replaces) clinical judgment

dimensions.label: Communication & Messaging
dimensions.description: Email, messaging, voice-of-Eleazar preservation, and relationship maintenance.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Communication is the #1 daily energy drain. After seeing patients all day, drafting personalized replies feels impossible. This dimension eliminates that friction while preserving authentic voice.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Self-determination theory (Deci & Ryan) shows authentic communication strengthens relationships. Studies show people can detect AI-generated writing—preserving voice requires learning individual communication patterns.
key_insights: Tone calibration per recipient improves relationship quality
Voice preservation requires continuous learning from corrections

dimensions.label: Family Coordination
dimensions.description: Family scheduling, activities, school communications, and relationship tracking.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Coordinating 3 kids across different activities creates logistical complexity. Missing a pickup time or school event has immediate family impact.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Family systems theory (Bowen) emphasizes that family is an emotional unit. Research shows parental stress directly impacts child wellbeing—reducing logistics burden improves family dynamics.
key_insights: School communication monitoring catches important notices

dimensions.label: Research & Education
dimensions.description: Medical research, continuing education, publications, and knowledge graph expansion.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Staying current with medical literature is both professional requirement and intellectual passion. Manual searching wastes time better spent on patient care or family.
dimensions.scientific_basis: The half-life of medical knowledge is ~5 years. Physicians need to process ~7,500 articles daily to stay current (Alper et al., 2004). AI-assisted filtering makes this tractable.
key_insights: Literature monitoring surfaces relevant papers automatically
Knowledge graph expansion creates serendipitous connections
Model evaluation ensures best tool for each task

dimensions.label: Health & Wellness
dimensions.description: Personal health, fitness, nutrition, sleep, and preventive care.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Physician, heal thyself. Doctors are notoriously bad at their own health. Proactive wellness prevents burnout and models healthy behavior for family.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Chronobiology research shows alignment with circadian rhythms improves cognitive performance by 20%. Sleep debt compounds—recovering from 1 hour lost takes 4+ nights.
key_insights: Sleep pattern analysis reveals optimization opportunities
Cognitive load monitoring predicts exhaustion before it hits
Family health coordination tracks pediatric schedules

dimensions.label: Financial Intelligence
dimensions.description: Investments, budgeting, financial planning, and wealth optimization.
dimensions.why_it_matters: MBA-level financial analysis without the spreadsheet time. Investment theses need tracking against market data to avoid emotional decisions.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Behavioral finance (Kahneman, Thaler) shows investors systematically make suboptimal decisions. Automated tracking removes emotion from portfolio management.
key_insights: Portfolio monitoring provides daily situational awareness
Thesis tracking prevents drift from investment rationale

dimensions.label: Goals & Milestones
dimensions.description: Personal and professional goal tracking, quarterly reviews, and career trajectory.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Without explicit goal tracking, day-to-day demands crowd out long-term priorities. Quarterly reviews prevent drift and maintain strategic focus.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Locke & Latham goal-setting theory: specific hard goals produce higher performance than easy goals. Progress tracking provides motivation through visible advancement.
key_insights: Career trajectory modeling informs skill development

dimensions.label: Infrastructure & Tech
dimensions.description: Homelab, tech stack, system maintenance, and AI infrastructure management.
dimensions.why_it_matters: The AI homelab is a force multiplier. Keeping systems running ensures all other dimensions have the computational foundation they need.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Reliability engineering (SRE principles) emphasizes that downtime has cascading effects. Proactive monitoring prevents the 2 AM wake-up call.
key_insights: Health monitoring catches issues before they cascade

dimensions.label: Metacognition
dimensions.description: Self-awareness, decision quality, bias detection, and thinking process optimization.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Thinking about thinking is the highest-leverage intervention. Understanding your own cognitive patterns lets you compensate for weaknesses.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Metacognition research (Flavell, 1979) shows experts have superior monitoring of their own comprehension. Training metacognitive skills improves learning and decision quality across domains.
key_insights: Bias detection surfaces systematic thinking errors
Cognitive pattern recognition reveals personal blind spots

dimensions.label: Decision Fatigue
dimensions.description: Cognitive load management, decision optimization, and ego depletion prevention.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Every decision depletes the same reservoir. Saving decisions for high-stakes choices preserves judgment when it matters most.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Baumeister's ego depletion research shows decision-making consumes self-regulatory resources. Strategic decision avoidance preserves capacity for important choices.

dimensions.label: Flow & Deep Work
dimensions.description: Deep work sessions, focus optimization, and flow state engineering.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Flow is the optimal state for high-value output. Protecting flow time from interruption is the highest-leverage time management intervention.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Csikszentmihalyi's flow research shows deep immersion produces both superior output and subjective wellbeing. Flow requires clear goals, immediate feedback, and challenge-skill balance.
key_insights: Flow state detection identifies optimal work windows

dimensions.label: Attention & Cognitive Load
dimensions.description: Cognitive load management, distraction filtering, and attention economy.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Attention is the scarcest resource. Every notification is a tax on cognitive capacity. Protecting attention is protecting productivity.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Attention economy (Simon, 1971): 'A wealth of information creates a poverty of attention.' Research shows it takes 23 minutes to refocus after interruption.

dimensions.label: Chronobiology
dimensions.description: Circadian rhythms, energy management, and temporal optimization.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Cognitive performance varies 20-30% across the day. Aligning task types with biological peaks maximizes output without extra effort.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Circadian research shows body temperature, cortisol, and alertness follow ~24-hour rhythms. Peak performance requires matching task demands to biological state.
key_insights: Energy mapping reveals personal peak hours
Light exposure optimization improves sleep quality

dimensions.label: Social Capital
dimensions.description: Relationship intelligence, network health, and social support optimization.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Social capital is the strongest predictor of wellbeing and career success. Nurturing relationships requires intentional effort that doesn't happen by accident.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Social network research (Granovetter) shows weak ties provide novel information. Putnam's 'Bowling Alone' documents decline in social capital—intentional cultivation counters this trend.
key_insights: Social support mapping identifies isolation risk

dimensions.label: Meaning & Purpose
dimensions.description: Purpose alignment, values clarification, and existential wellbeing.
dimensions.why_it_matters: Meaning is the ultimate meta-question. Without clarity on purpose, other optimizations feel empty. Viktor Frankl: 'Man's main concern is not to gain pleasure or to avoid pain but rather to see a meaning in his life.'
dimensions.scientific_basis: Logotherapy research (Frankl) shows meaning-seeking is fundamental human motivation. Positive psychology (Seligman) identifies meaning as key component of wellbeing beyond pleasure.

dimensions.label: Habit Architecture
dimensions.description: Behavior design, habit tracking, and routine optimization.
dimensions.why_it_matters: 40% of daily actions are habits, not decisions. Optimizing habit architecture is the highest-leverage behavior change strategy.
dimensions.scientific_basis: Duhigg's habit loop: cue → routine → reward. Wood & Neal research shows habits persist because they become neurologically efficient. James's 'laws of habit' provide design principles.
